name: pricing-tier
role: Single pricing-tier card. Composes from sibling pattern packs: <action-button> from @quoin/pattern-button-system for the tier CTA; <form-control type='switch'> from @quoin/pattern-form-fields for in-card option toggles when present. Four canonical variants — starter (free, low-commitment), default (paid baseline), featured (highlighted 'most popular' tier with accent border + 1px ring), enterprise (custom CTA, 'Talk to sales' instead of price). All 6 states + 8 microstates.

semanticStructure: <article> — the tier card; aria-labelledby points to the tier-name <h3>
<header> — tier name + tagline + optional 'most popular' badge
<h3> — tier name (e.g. 'Pro', 'Team', 'Enterprise'); rendered tracking-wide uppercase at type-size-sm
<div class='price'> — the price block; <span class='amount'> + <span class='unit'> + <span class='cadence'>. Tabular numerics enforced.
— short value-proposition sentence below the price
<ul class='features'> — bullet list of included features; each <li> opens with a checkmark or X icon (X for excluded features in a comparison view)
<footer> — the CTA. Composes <action-button> from @quoin/pattern-button-system with intent=primary for featured tier, intent=secondary for others, intent=link for enterprise 'Talk to sales'.

variants.starter: Free / $0 tier. CTA is 'Get started' (intent=secondary). No featured highlight. Minimal feature list. Tagline emphasises 'no card required'.
variants.default: Paid baseline tier. CTA is 'Start free trial' (intent=secondary). Standard card chrome. Full feature list.
variants.featured: Highlighted 'most popular' tier. CTA is 'Start free trial' (intent=primary). Accent-coloured border at --border-width-md plus 1px outer ring via box-shadow. Optional 'MOST POPULAR' badge in header.
variants.enterprise: Custom pricing tier. No numeric price; replace amount block with 'Custom' or 'Let's talk'. CTA is 'Talk to sales' (intent=link or intent=primary depending on conversion strategy). Tagline emphasises white-glove onboarding + SLA + dedicated CSM.

meta.billingCadence: When billing=monthly the price block shows '$X / mo' with tabular numerics. When billing=annual the price block shows '$X / mo' with a subscript-style '$Y billed annually' caption below. Annual usually shows 17-20% savings vs monthly; surface as 'Save 17% · billed annually' note when toggling.
meta.comparisonMode: When the parent pricing-grid has state='comparison-mode', tiers render in a comparison-table layout instead of card-grid. Each tier becomes a column; features become rows. Tier card chrome (border, padding, shadow) recedes; the row structure carries the visual weight.
meta.loadingPattern: skeleton lines: 1 short line for tier name, 1 long line for price, 3-5 lines for features, 1 line for CTA. data-state=loading + aria-busy=true.
meta.checkoutInitiatedPattern: After tier CTA click. The CTA shows loading state (delegates to action-button's [aria-busy] microstate). Other tier CTAs disable to prevent double-submission. aria-live='polite' region announces 'Redirecting to checkout…'.

ariaPatterns.tierLabelledby: <article aria-labelledby='<tier-name-id>'> wires the entire tier to its name heading
ariaPatterns.mostPopularBadge: If present, badge has aria-label='Most popular plan' — text content is the visual treatment ('MOST POPULAR'), aria-label is what screen readers announce in context
ariaPatterns.comparisonTableSemantics: In comparison-mode, render as semantic <table> with <thead> tiers + <tbody> feature rows. Don't fake table structure with divs.
ariaPatterns.annualMonthlyToggle: The billing toggle uses <form-control type='switch'> from pattern-form-fields. role='switch' + aria-checked. Toggle change announces price changes via aria-live region.

name: pricing-grid
role: Composition primitive arranging multiple pricing-tier cards. Three layout modes: 3-tier (canonical starter+pro+enterprise; the highlighted middle tier is featured), 4-tier (starter+pro+team+enterprise; second-from-left or second-from-right is featured), table (comparison matrix with tiers as columns + features as rows). Inherits density from parent.

layouts.3-tier: grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr) at ≥1024px; 1fr below 1024px (vertical stack). Featured tier visually elevated via translateY(-8px) at ≥1024px, returning to flat on mobile.
layouts.4-tier: grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr) at ≥1280px; repeat(2, 1fr) at 768–1279px; 1fr below 768px.
layouts.table: rendered as <table> with thead (tier headers) + tbody (feature rows). Sticky thead during scroll. Features grouped by category via <tbody> + caption rows.

meta.billingToggleHost: pricing-grid hosts the billing toggle in its <header> region. The toggle is a <form-control type='switch'> from @quoin/pattern-form-fields. When toggled, all child pricing-tier cards' billing attribute changes synchronously; aria-live announces the cadence change.

name: billing-toggle
role: Annual/monthly billing cadence switcher. Composition wrapper around the form-fields pattern's <form-control type='switch'> primitive. Two visual treatments — switch (toggle UI per WAI-ARIA 1.3) and segmented (button-group from button-system pattern in connected register). The segmented variant uses radio semantics; the switch variant uses checkbox-with-role-switch semantics.

meta.savingsBadge: When current=annual, render a small 'Save 17%' badge next to the toggle in --success colour. The savings figure should be calculated from the actual tier pricing, not hardcoded.

ariaPatterns.switchVariant: role='switch' + aria-checked='true|false' on the inner <input>. Change announced via aria-live='polite'.
ariaPatterns.segmentedVariant: role='radiogroup' on the wrapper; role='radio' + aria-checked on each button. Use the button-group connected register from pattern-button-system.
